passage: The software update was pushed to all devices automatically during the overnight maintenance window. Without requiring any action from the end users, who would see only a brief notification upon their next login. Most users did not even notice the update had occurred.
question: Which choice best corrects the sentence structure error in the passage?

choices.A: The software update was pushed to all devices automatically during the overnight maintenance window, without requiring any action from the end users, who would see only a brief notification upon their next login.
choices.B: The software update was pushed to all devices automatically during the overnight maintenance window; without requiring any action from the end users, who would see only a brief notification.
choices.C: Without requiring any action, the software update was pushed to all devices automatically during the overnight maintenance window. Users would see only a brief notification.
choices.D: No change; 'Without requiring any action' begins a complete sentence that specifies how the update was delivered.

explanation: The second sentence 'Without requiring any action from the end users, who would see only a brief notification upon their next login' is a prepositional phrase fragment. It begins with 'Without,' a preposition, and has no independent subject-verb structure—it cannot stand alone as a sentence. This phrase modifies the update process described in the first sentence and should be attached to it. Choice A attaches the fragment with a comma after the main clause. Choice B uses a semicolon before the fragment, which is incorrect for a prepositional phrase. Choice C moves the phrase to the front of the sentence and makes structural changes. Choice D incorrectly defends the fragment.
